title: picris hieracioides subsp. grandiflora
intro: Picris hieracioides subsp. grandiflora, also known as the Grand-leaved Hawkweed or Picris, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Picris hieracioides subsp. grandiflora include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies to support the immune system and reduce inflammation.
